description: Cross-domain rejection corpus (plan section 3.3): artifacts produced under legacy Ascent-era domain separation must FAIL NOOSPHERE verification. Every legacy identity string is hex-encoded (old_*_hex fields) so no forbidden literal enters the tree; decode the hex to reproduce the legacy construction.

cases.name: old-bls-attest-dst-rejected-by-noos-vote
cases.why: BLS signature hashed to G2 under the legacy attest DST (old_dst_hex); verification under every NOOS BLS_DST row must fail

cases.name: old-bls-beacon-dst-rejected-by-noos-dkg
cases.why: BLS signature under the legacy beacon DST (old_dst_hex); verification under D-BLS-DKG (and all other NOOS DSTs) must fail

cases.name: old-derive-key-domain-hash-mismatch
cases.why: legacy chain hashed with keyed BLAKE3 under derive_key(old_context_hex, old_key_material_hex); the NOOS D-TX-ID digest of the same payload must differ

cases.name: old-ed25519-raw-message-rejected-by-noos-sig-tx
cases.why: legacy chain signed the raw message with no context prefix; NOOS D-SIG-TX verification prepends the registered context and must fail

cases.name: old-ed25519-legacy-prefix-rejected-by-noos-sig-tx
cases.why: signature binds a legacy uppercase object prefix (old_prefix_hex); NOOS D-SIG-TX verification must fail
